For this incident, Mosley was initially charged with five counts of criminal vehicular homicide.
She opted to avoid a jury trial and instead have a judge make a decision about her guilt.

During the bench trial, a Ramsey County judge said prosecutors didn't meet the burden of proof for three of those offenses and Mosley was found guilty of two counts of criminal vehicular homicide in January 2024.
The judge spared Mosley from what could have been a multi-year prison sentence.
She was instead ordered to serve nine months in jail and five years of probation, along with treatment requirements and restitution of $7,500.
All the evidence, including her high speeds and combined alcohol and marijuana use, turned Mosley's routine drive into a fatal crash with irreversible consequences.
This case shows the deadly combination of impaired driving and reckless speed.
Mosley's actions not only took the life of her passenger, but had long-lasting repercussions for family members, friends, and the witnesses of the events that morning.
Her passenger, 19-year-old Shaterius Barlow of Minneapolis, died at the scene.
Up until now, this video has never been seen outside of a courtroom.
